title: Slow-Cooked Smoky Dutch Pork Tenderloin Hash
description: A hearty, creamy Dutch dish featuring slow-cooked pork tenderloin, perfect for meal prep. Can be made in a vessel like a tava griddle.
instructions: Preheat the tava griddle or a large non-stick skillet to medium-low heat.
Season the pork tenderloin with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in the griddle and sear the pork tenderloin until browned.
Remove the pork from the griddle and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the griddle and cook the diced onion until softened.
Add the diced carrot and potato to the griddle and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the griddle and cook for 1 minute.
Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually add the chicken broth to the griddle, whisking continuously.
Return the pork tenderloin to the griddle and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the pork from the griddle and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Stir in the milk and grated cheddar until the cheese is melted and the mixture is creamy.
Slice the pork tenderloin and serve with the hash.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F, then rest 3 minutes before serving.
